trivia: The C-130 Hercules first flew in 1954 and has never stopped being built, the longest continuous military aircraft production run in history. Tactical airlift, gunship, tanker, firefighter, hurricane hunter: the airframe does everything.

This identity covers the classic Hercules generations through the C-130H, the ones your antenna hears on air forces worldwide. The modern C-130J squawks its own code and will join the catalog as its own type.
